channel.title: Seafood on paper.archive
channel.description: Recent content in Seafood on paper.archive

item.title: Yasik — Sea Snail Salad & Blanched Squid, Delivered
item.description:
Late-night eating is practically a national sport in Korea — it even has its own word, yasik (야식). Tonight’s yasik came by delivery app.

Sora-muchim (소라무침) — sea snail tossed with minari and perilla leaves in a sweet-tangy gochujang dressing. Chewy, spicy, bright. I boiled a batch of somyeon (소면, thin wheat noodles) myself to go with it: mixing the noodles into the leftover dressing is honestly the main event.

item.title: Hanchi Mulhoe & Octopus Rice Bowl — Yoonsikdang, Geoje
item.description:
Lunch on Geoje Island at a place called Yoonsikdang. We ordered hanchi mulhoe and a rock-octopus rice bowl.

Hanchi mulhoe

Mulhoe (물회) is one of Korea’s great summer seafood dishes: raw seafood tossed in a sweet-tangy gochujang dressing, then submerged in icy broth — basically a cold, drinkable raw-fish salad. Hanchi (한치) is a small squid prized for its tender crunch. On a hot day this is hard to beat.
